frameworks.name: active inference
frameworks.one_line: Perception combines prior expectations with noisy sensory measurements

frameworks.name: sensory coding schemes
frameworks.one_line: Competing encoding proposals tested against measured neural tuning

frameworks.name: natural image statistics prior
frameworks.one_line: Environmental prior embedded into early cortical measurement

core_question: How does the human visual system neurally instantiate prior expectations for orientation?
key_assumptions: Perception is active inference combining priors with noisy measurements
Environmental statistics can be expressed as a prior over orientation
Neural tuning is recoverable via data-driven extraction
manipulations: Data-driven extraction of orientation representation from the visual system
Comparison of measured tuning with multiple candidate encoding schemes
key_findings: Tuning of the human visual system is highly conditional on stimulus-specific variations
Observed tuning is not predicted by previous encoding proposals
Adopted encoding scheme embeds an environmental prior into the sensory measurement
interpretations: Priors for natural image statistics are realised within early cortical encoding itself
This functional architecture supports optimal inference at the earliest stages of cortical processing
